core_mechanism: An undirected conditional-independence graphical model (Markov random field) whose dependence structure is estimated in the joint extreme tails via multivariate extreme-value theory (a Hüsler-Reiss extremal graph), so that graph edges encode direct pairwise coupling after conditioning out linkages mediated through the rest of the system
brief_reason: The abstract's method is a 'dynamic Hüsler-Reiss graphical model of extremes' that maps conditional dependence 'directly in the joint tails,' isolating direct extremal linkages from those mediated by the rest of the system and benchmarked against a Gaussian graphical model — i.e. a tail-restricted conditional-independence graph.
